title: Persist created projects in the project list (staging vs source read model)
description:
On Save (mh3.5), the new project is optimistically prepended to the project list, but it VANISHES ON RELOAD: writes go to the staging DB (data/export/), while the read path (/api/projects) loads the source working copy, so the new project isn't there on the next fetch. The user noticed and expects saved projects to stay in the list.

Decide the read/write model so created projects persist:
- Option A: have the app READ from the staging DB once it exists (shows source-clone + your exports in one view) — but staging is a one-time clone of source, so existing projects can go stale vs the live DB.
- Option B: enable LIVE writes by default (currently env-gated via TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE, not exposed in UI) so the normal list (reads source) reflects new projects. Needs UI exposure + confirmation; live writes already have backup + BEGIN IMMEDIATE lock-detect + additive contract.
- Option C: keep staging but add an explicit 'import staging into source/NINA' action + refresh.

RESOLUTION CHOSEN (2026-06-19): Option B, via o2c's live-write toggle. The reader re-snapshots source on every read (working_copy.py get_working_copy refresh=True), so a live write shows up on the next /api/projects fetch. Implement the staging-vs-live write-target toggle (UI choice + confirm) as part of o2c (Phase 4); close 2ij when that lands. See [[ts-assistant-codebase]] export.py/EXPORT_DB. Discovered during mh3.5 testing.
close_reason: Resolved by live mode (PR #15): in live mode read target == write target, so created projects persist across reload (Option B). Staging keeps its by-design session-local caveat, documented.

title: Staging export DB goes stale: re-seed when source DB changes
description: The staging export DB (data/export/schedulerdb-export.sqlite) is cloned from the source ONCE (_seed_export_db only clones when the file is absent) and then persists/accumulates across sessions. If the source DB changes (user swaps it) or stale/foreign rows accumulate (e.g. cross-session/parallel writes, or rows from before a writer fix), the staging file diverges from the source and can contain invalid rows. Real impact (2026-06-20): a user's exported staging DB contained 2 projects with NULL createDate (one not even in the source) left from older writes; NINA's GetAllProjects throws ConstraintException on load. Fix idea: re-seed staging from source when the source signature changes (backup_signature already tracks this), or expose a 'reset staging' action, or always re-clone before an export when staging is older than source. Workaround applied: moved the polluted file aside so it re-seeds. Hardened o2c update_project to COALESCE-backfill NINA-required columns so edits self-heal NULLs.
close_reason: Superseded by ur0 (PR #26): no staging export DB exists anymore, so it can't go stale. Writes are in place.

title: Exposure plan groups — named reusable bundles (qiz.1 Stage 3)
description: Stage 3 of 3 (the big one). New TS-Assistant concept absent from NINA's schema: a named 'exposure plan group' bundling several exposure templates + desired counts (e.g. 'LRGB Dark Nebula', 'SHO Bright Target'), applied to a target in one pick. Stored app-side (store/plan_templates.py -> data/plan_templates.json, equipment.py pattern); PlanGroup { id, name, items:[{exposure_template_id, desired}] }; CRUD GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/plan-templates. Frontend: group manager + 'Apply plan group' dropdown that expands into editable exposure plans. Writer/export unchanged (groups expand to plans before Save). No NINA schema change.
close_reason: Merged in PR #12 (exposure plan groups: app-side store + CRUD + manager panel + apply-in-builder).

title: Evaluate removing custom survey dropdown in favor of Aladin's built-in layers control
description: Aladin Lite v3 has a built-in layers/stack control for choosing the image survey, overlapping our custom topbar dropdown (fed by /api/surveys). Evaluate whether the built-in control is functionally equivalent; if so, remove our dropdown (App.tsx survey picker, possibly /api/surveys). Nuance: our NSNS narrowband HiPS (simg.de/nebulae3/dr0_1/*) and the CORS-pinned DSS URL are NOT in Aladin's default registry, so to keep them available in the built-in control we'd register them via aladin.newImageSurvey/createImageSurvey/addNewImageSurvey so they show in the layers list. Decision: remove dropdown + register NSNS into Aladin's control, or keep the dropdown. Files: frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/sky/AladinView.tsx, backend/app/api/surveys.py.
close_reason: Evaluated empirically: Aladin's built-in Stack control lists only a fixed CDS registry (DSS, 2MASS, PanSTARRS, SDSS9, VTSS-Ha, ...) and does NOT include NSNS; createImageSurvey() registers a survey object but does not inject it into that selectable list. Built-in control is NOT functionally equivalent to our curated dropdown (one-click NSNS + CORS-pinned DSS), and it is complementary (also hosts overlay styling + add-HiPS-by-URL). Decision: KEEP the dropdown. Reopen if we later invest in registering NSNS into the Stack UI.

title: Author filter cadence + override exposure order (per-target ordering)
description: Build per-target authoring UIs for the two complex Target Scheduler ordering features deferred from psq: filtercadenceitem (filter cadence) and overrideexposureorderitem (override exposure order). These are ordered, positional (referenceIdx) per-target lists needing bespoke drag-order editors + write paths to those tables. Completing this also unblocks o2c editing of projects that already have cadence/override rows (o2c v1 refuses them). Child of qiz / follows psq.
notes: Implemented in PR #29 (Override Exposure Order authoring; filter cadence left to NINA per FSF). Backend 85 green; E2E 9/9; live round-trip confirmed. o2c now edits projects with cadence/override. Real-NINA import round-trip pending owner check.
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #29; real-NINA import round-trip confirmed by owner. Override Exposure Order authoring (expose/dither steps) + o2c unblocked for cadence/override projects. Filter cadence left to NINA (FSF-derived). Backend 85 green; E2E 9/9.

title: Named-object markers should be non-interactive labels (no hover highlight, no click)
description:
Named-object decorations in the sky view should be passive labels only — no mouseover highlight and not clickable.

Current state (frontend/src/sky/AladinView.tsx): named objects are drawn as a separate Aladin catalog (A.catalog name 'Named objects', shape 'circle', sourceSize 5, displayLabel true, labelColumn 'label', onClick 'showPopup') plus a green extent ring overlay (namedCircles, #7dffb0). Because it is a catalog with onClick 'showPopup', the markers highlight on hover and open a popup on click. The objectClicked handler routes real target markers by obj.data.id; named objects (no id) fall through to closePopup, but the catalog's own showPopup still fires.

Desired: render the named-object names as inert text labels (and keep the extent ring), with no hover highlighting and no click interaction.

Implementation notes / options to evaluate:
- Remove onClick 'showPopup' from the 'Named objects' catalog (set to none) so clicks do not open a popup.
- Aladin Lite catalog markers hover-highlight by design; if there is no flag to disable it, options include: hide the marker glyph (shape 'cross'/size 0 or transparent) and keep only the label, and/or disable pointer events on that catalog's canvas layer so it cannot capture hover/click.
- Ensure interactive elements are unaffected: the target FOV markers/boxes must stay clickable (recenter/select) and empty-sky click-to-dismiss must still work; only the named-object layer becomes non-interactive.
- Verify the named-object label still respects the zoom-aware declutter (syncNamed).

title: Profile-scoped everything: make NINA profile a top-level selector
description:
Target Scheduler is multi-profile: each NINA profile has its own projects, targets, and exposure templates. TS Assistant should model this too, with PROFILE as a top-level concept the user selects, scoping everything beneath it.

Make these all local to a specific profile (not global):
- Rigs / equipment (currently app-side equipment.json is global) -> per profile.
- Projects (and their targets) -> filtered/created under the active profile.
- Exposure templates (qiz.1) -> listed/created within the active profile (exposuretemplate.profileId).
- Exposure plan groups (qiz.1 Stage 3, app-side) -> tagged with profileId, only shown for the active profile.

Implications:
- A top-level profile picker in the UI (read distinct profileId from the DB; let the user choose the active one). Replaces the current ad-hoc 'NINA profile' free-text field in the Project builder and the global equipment list.
- Backend reads/writes filter by profileId; app-side stores (equipment, plan groups) gain a profileId key.
- New projects/templates inherit the active profile automatically.

Relates to: qiz.1 (exposure templates carry profileId), o2c (editing must stay within a profile), and the equipment store. Discovered 2026-06-19 — user wants profile to be the top-level organizing axis, mirroring Target Scheduler.
notes: Stage 3/3 (alias-rename + 'NINA Profile' label) in PR (branch feat/profile-alias-rename, off Stage 2): inline rename in ProfilePicker -> setProfileAlias -> PUT /api/profiles/{id}; App.onRenameProfile updates profileList. Build+lint clean; E2E verify-profile-scoping 11/11 (incl. rename). All 3 stages done pending merges.
close_reason: All 3 stages shipped & merged: PR #21 (backend foundation), #22 (topbar picker + scoping), #23 (alias-rename + 'NINA Profile' label). Profile is now the top-level organizing axis scoping projects/templates/plan-templates/equipment. Backend 67 green; E2E verify-profile-scoping 11/11.

title: Scroll sidebar to top when editing a project
description: Clicking Edit on a draft loads it into the builder at the top of the Projects panel, but if the list was scrolled down there's no visible indication editing began. Scroll the sidebar to the top on Edit so the builder (edit mode) is visible. Implemented alongside m74/hve in the exposure-order UI-polish PR.
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #30. Drag-and-drop exposure order (with full-row drag image + drop indicator), smart-order conflict warning, and scroll-to-top on Edit.

title: Named-object overlay: circle + label well-known/large objects on the sky view
description: Add an Aladin overlay that draws circles (sized to the object's angular extent) around larger / well-known deep-sky objects (e.g. Messier/NGC/IC catalog highlights) and labels them with their common/catalog names, so the user can orient and find framing targets at a glance. Frontend-only (Aladin graphicOverlay + labels/markers, like the existing FOV/draft overlays). Source the object list from a bundled catalog or a HiPS/MOC/VizieR query; togglable like the FOV-boxes layer. Future enhancement to the sky view.
close_reason: Named-object overlay shipped: bundled curated catalog (41 Messier/NGC/IC highlights) → extent circles + labels, topbar 'Named objects' toggle, verified via verify-cia.mjs

title: Creating exposure template fails with 400 (e.g. 'L_900s_Gain56' based on 'L_900s')
description:
Repro (production): tried to create an exposure template named 'L_900s_Gain56' based on existing 'L_900s'. Backend returned 400: POST /api/exposure-templates -> 400 Bad Request.

Diagnosis so far (backend/app/api/templates.py error mapping): ValidationError -> 422, DatabaseBusyError/ProgressError -> 409, ExportError -> 400. So this is specifically an ExportError raised in create_exposure_template (backend/app/db/export.py). The real message is in the 400 RESPONSE BODY ('detail'), which the access log does not show — capturing it is step one.

Where ExportError can come from in create_exposure_template:
- _resolve_target: no source DB (unlikely here — reads work).
- 'quick_check failed before write' — pre-existing DB integrity issue.
- PRAGMA foreign_key_check returns violations — NOTE this checks the WHOLE database, so any pre-existing orphaned/inconsistent row (common in real long-lived NINA DBs) would abort an otherwise-valid create with a 400. Strong candidate.
- additive check / non-additive id reuse guards.
- _busy_or_export_error: a non-busy sqlite3.OperationalError during write_exposure_template -> ExportError(str(e)) -> 400 (a lock/busy would be 409 instead).

Ruled out: schema drift (missing exposuretemplate column) would surface as 422 — validate_exposure_template calls _check_columns against TEMPLATE_WRITTEN_COLUMNS, which exactly matches the INSERT column list in write_exposure_template. NOTE: an IntegrityError (UNIQUE/NOT NULL) is NOT a subclass of OperationalError, so it would NOT map to 400 — it would propagate as 500; since we saw 400, a UNIQUE-name collision is unlikely to be the cause (but worth confirming there is no UNIQUE(profileId,name) constraint).

Action items:
1. Reproduce and capture the 400 response JSON 'detail' (browser devtools Network tab, or curl the endpoint) — that pinpoints which ExportError fired.
2. If it is foreign_key_check/quick_check on the whole DB: decide whether create should be blocked by pre-existing, unrelated inconsistencies, or whether the integrity gate should be scoped/relaxed for additive single-row inserts (note ur0 set every write to backup + quick_check). Tune accordingly.
3. UX: surface the ExportError 'detail' in the frontend (createExport/template create path) so the user sees WHY it failed instead of a generic failure. Confirm frontend (api.ts createExposureTemplate + ProjectBuilder/template UI) shows server error text.
4. Add a regression test once the cause is known.

Minor: templates.py + export.py docstrings still say writes go to a 'staging copy' — stale post-ur0 (single in-place mode); fix while here.

title: Optional Docker / docker-compose deployment
description:
Provide an optional containerized deployment so users can run TS Assistant via `docker compose up` instead of hand-managing a uv backend + Vite frontend.

WHY: Lowers the setup bar (no local Python/uv/Node toolchain) and gives a reproducible, single-command way to run the app next to NINA — making it easier on non-developer astrophotographers. Strictly optional; the existing bare-metal run path stays first-class.

SCOPE:
- Backend image: Python 3.11+ (uv-based build), FastAPI/uvicorn on :8008.
- Frontend: build the Vite/React app to static assets; serve them (nginx or uvicorn static) — or run vite preview. Expose :5173 (or chosen port).
- docker-compose.yml wiring both services; expose both ports.
- .dockerignore (exclude node_modules, .venv, data/, .git, *.mjs scratch, etc.).
- Document in README under a clear 'Run with Docker (optional)' section.

KEY CONSTRAINTS / GOTCHAS (this app is unusual):
- The user's REAL Target Scheduler DB must be bind-mounted in. Set TS_ASSISTANT_DB to the in-container path. In STAGING mode it's read-only; in LIVE mode (TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE=1) the backend reads+writes it directly AND writes -wal/-shm sidecars + a backup — so the mount must be read-write and on the same filesystem semantics. Mount the DB's *directory*, not just the file, so SQLite WAL sidecars work.
- Persist data/ (working copies, data/export, data/backups, equipment.json/plan_templates.json) to a named volume or host bind so backups + app state survive container restarts. Live-mode backups are ~158MB each × keep_last — document the disk requirement (see o2c/backup design).
- Frontend resolves the API as http://<window.location.hostname>:8008 — so for remote access the published backend port must match what the browser can reach, or set VITE_API_BASE at build time. Document this.
- Honor the launch-time mode env (TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE) via compose environment; default STAGING (safe). No in-app switch — mode is fixed at container launch, consistent with o2c.
- Likely run as a non-root container user; ensure that user can read (and in live mode write) the bind-mounted DB — document UID/permission guidance.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `docker compose up` brings up backend :8008 + frontend; /api/health responds and reports the expected mode.
- Pointing TS_ASSISTANT_DB at a mounted DB lists projects; STAGING by default.
- LIVE mode with a rw mount writes + backs up to the persisted data/ volume; WAL sidecars handled.
- README documents both modes, the DB/data mounts, ports, and the disk-space note.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #18: optional Docker/compose deployment (two-service, validated by build + smoke test).

title: Drag-and-drop reordering for the custom exposure order editor
description: The Override Exposure Order editor (OverrideOrderEditor, awh) currently reorders steps via up/down arrow buttons, which are tedious. Enhancement: replace with draggable handles on each row so exposures and dithers can be dragged/dropped into order. Frontend-only; keep keyboard-accessible fallback. Discovered 2026-06-20 by owner after awh shipped.
notes: Done (PR pending): drag-and-drop handles replace the up/down arrows in OverrideOrderEditor.
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #30. Drag-and-drop exposure order (with full-row drag image + drop indicator), smart-order conflict warning, and scroll-to-top on Edit.

title: Reader connections never closed -> stale reads until backend restart when NINA writes
description:
Symptom (reported from production): after NINA modified schedulerdb.sqlite, the web UI reloaded but showed stale data; only restarting the backend container picked up the change.

Root cause: read paths used 'with connect_readonly(wc) as conn:'. Python's sqlite3 Connection context manager does NOT close the connection on exit — it only ends the transaction. So every read request (/projects, /targets, /equipment, ...) opened a new read handle that was never closed; they accumulate for the process lifetime. With read handles persistently open, the process keeps its WAL index mapped and serves a cached view; when an external writer (NINA, a separate process and on a typical rig a separate host over an SMB/NFS mount where SQLite WAL coherence is unsupported) commits new frames, the backend doesn't re-read them until the process restarts and re-opens everything. Not reproduced in dev because there is no concurrent external writer and local-FS WAL stays coherent.

Fix: close read connections after each read. Wrapped all 5 read sites in contextlib.closing() (reader.py x4, introspect.py x1) and documented the requirement on connect_readonly() in working_copy.py. Closing per read forces each request to re-open and rescan the -wal, picking up the latest committed data. Note write paths (export.py), the startup probe (main.py), and backup.py already close correctly.

Relationship: distinct from kfc (UI auto-refresh feature). Even with this fixed, the UI still only re-fetches on load/user action; kfc remains valid for live auto-refresh.

Verification: 89 backend tests pass. Real check is production: change a project in NINA, reload the UI (no container restart) -> change is reflected.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #32 (merged): read connections now closed via contextlib.closing; verified in production — NINA changes appear on UI reload without a container restart.

title: Exposure plan filter enabled/disabled toggle: read, display, and persist
description:
Support and display NINA's per-filter enabled/disabled state on a target's exposure plans, and persist changes the user makes.

Current state (gap):
- DB column exists: exposureplan.enabled INTEGER DEFAULT 1.
- WRITE: writer.py hardcodes enabled to 1 on INSERT ('VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?)'); ExposurePlanSpec has no 'enabled' field, so every created plan is forced enabled.
- READ: models.ExposurePlan and reader.py do NOT load 'enabled'; frontend api.ts ExposurePlan has no 'enabled' field. So it is never surfaced.

Scope:
1. Read: add 'enabled: bool' to backend models.ExposurePlan (default True) + load it in reader.py (read 'enabled', default 1); add to api.ts ExposurePlan.
2. Display: show an enabled/disabled toggle (checkbox) per filter row in the exposure-plan UI; visually de-emphasize disabled plans.
3. Persist on create + edit: add 'enabled' to ExposurePlanSpec and write the real value instead of the hardcoded 1, in both write_project and the o2c update_project plan-rebuild path; include it in ExportRequest.
4. Decide the write trigger: either fold into the existing project Save (export PUT) — consistent with current edit flow — or add a lightweight direct toggle endpoint for changing it inline without a full project edit. User asked to 'support writing enabled/disabled state as they are changed by the user', which leans toward an inline toggle; evaluate against the in-place write+backup path (every write takes a backup) so rapid toggles do not spam backups.

Notes:
- NINA's UI lets you enable/disable individual exposure plans; mirrors that.
- Verify round-trip: disabled plan reads back disabled; NINA honors the flag.
- overrideexposureorderitem references plans by index — confirm disabling a plan does not desync override-order indices.

title: Live/production operating mode (read+write the real Target Scheduler DB)
description:
PR1 of the o2c split (user decision 2026-06-20): add a launch-time operating mode where the app reads AND writes the user's real Target Scheduler DB directly, instead of reading a snapshot and writing a staging copy. Selected by the existing TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE env var (no in-app switch). Routes the EXISTING additive flows (create project, create template, undo) at the live DB; no UPDATE/DELETE here (that's o2c). Resolves 2ij for live users (created rows persist on reload because read target == write target).

Single chokepoint: config.resolve_active_mode() cached at startup -> {name, source, read_path, write_target, integrity_default, backup_window_min, live_error}; both working_copy.get_working_copy and export._resolve_target consult it so read/write can never desync.

Live-DB safety must-fixes (from safety review): (1) WAL-visible reads (mode=ro on a WAL DB NINA holds open can miss committed frames) -> open rw-by-discipline or PASSIVE-checkpoint first; (2) WAL-consistent backups (same issue in consistent_copy); (3) -wal size+mtime in backup_signature + backup_window_min=0 in live so coalescing can't reuse a pre-NINA-change backup (data-loss-on-restore); (4) run integrity_check OUTSIDE the held write lock in live (don't block NINA mid-capture on 158MB); (5) keep BEGIN IMMEDIATE busy->409 fail-fast, clearer message; (6) gzip backups in live. live+no-source -> live_error, never silent staging fallback. /api/health reports mode; UI shows persistent PRODUCTION banner.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #15 (live/production operating mode); merged to upstream main 1fbe59f

title: Auto-detect external DB changes and refresh the UI
description:
UI auto-refresh feature. NOTE: a separate backend stale-read BUG (ij9) was found and fixed — read connections were never closed so the server served a cached view until restart. With ij9 fixed the backend returns fresh data per request, but the running UI still only re-fetches on page load or user action (no polling/WebSocket/file-watch/focus refetch). This feature is to make the open UI notice external DB changes and refresh on its own.

Possible approaches (to evaluate):
- Lightweight client polling of a cheap backend change-token endpoint (PRAGMA data_version is more reliable than file mtime under NINA's WAL); re-fetch when it changes; optional refetch on window focus/visibilitychange.
- Backend SSE/WebSocket pushing a 'db-changed' event from a data_version/mtime watcher.
- Manual 'Refresh' button as a minimum viable step.
The /api/health endpoint (app/main.py) could carry the change token.

title: Create new exposure templates (qiz.1 Stage 2)
description: Stage 2 of 3. Let the user create new exposure templates (full columns: filtername, gain, offset, bin, readoutmode, twilightlevel, moonavoidance*, dither, minutesOffset, defaultexposure). Backend: ExposureTemplateSpec + writer.write_exposure_template (full-column INSERT + guid) + export.create_exposure_template through the existing backup+BEGIN IMMEDIATE+provenance+_assert_additive wrapper (one INSERT, additive & undoable) + POST /api/exposure-templates. Frontend: template editor create form; on save refresh list + auto-select.
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #13 (commit 4541795 'feat(l1b/qiz.5): create new exposure templates') with follow-ups 2d452f5/89c33d1. Backend: ExposureTemplateSpec + write_exposure_template (full-col INSERT) + export.create_exposure_template (additive/provenanced/undoable) + POST /api/exposure-templates. Frontend: NewTemplateModal create form, auto-select on save. Backend suite 59 green.

title: Warn when a custom exposure order is set but Smart Exposure Order is on
description: Smart Exposure Selection (smartexposureorder) overrides a custom Override Exposure Order — they're mutually exclusive in effect (not harmful, but the custom order is ignored). Enhancement: show a small warning text under the Smart Exposure Order checkbox (Advanced settings) when the project has a non-empty Override Exposure Order AND smart_exposure_order is true, telling the user the custom order will be ignored. Frontend-only (ProjectSettingsEditor / OverrideOrderEditor coordination). Discovered 2026-06-20 by owner after awh shipped.
notes: Done (PR pending): warning under Smart Exposure Order when a custom exposure order is set + smart order on.
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #30. Drag-and-drop exposure order (with full-row drag image + drop indicator), smart-order conflict warning, and scroll-to-top on Edit.

title: P3 — Database write / export path
description: Create Project + panel Targets + ExposurePlans in the DB safely: timestamped backup, schema/FK/EF validation, transactional write. Highest risk.
close_reason: P3 complete: mh3.1 (schema round-trip) → mh3.2 (backup+transactional additive writer) → mh3.3 (validation) → mh3.4 (export API) → mh3.5 (frontend Save). Framing→save→import-into-NINA loop works; writes to safe staging, additive-only + backup + undo + progress gate.

title: Schema round-trip fidelity test (gating)
description: Write a Project+Targets+ExposurePlans set, diff against a NINA-written DB to prove fidelity incl. __EFMigrationsHistory expectations. Gates the writer.
close_reason: Round-trip fidelity test landed: canonical schema module (byte-identical to NINA), faithful writer, 7 passing tests proving schema + data + RA-hours + FK fidelity and NO __EFMigrationsHistory/sqlite_sequence

title: Backup + transactional writer
description: Auto timestamped backup before write; transactional insert of Project + panel Targets + ExposurePlans matching exact schema.
close_reason: Backup + transactional additive-only writer landed: export.py orchestrator (BEGIN IMMEDIATE, FK+additive+integrity assertions, COMMIT/ROLLBACK), time-coalesced backups (backup.py), in-DB provenance table + progress gate (provenance.py), app-side ops bookkeeping (ops.py), validation seam (validate.py), undo_operation. Live-write opt-in gated. 12 tests, full suite 27 green.

title: Write validation (schema/FK/EF integrity)
description: Validate against introspected schema, FK integrity, and EF concurrency/migration constraints before committing.
close_reason: Write validation deepened: validate_schema (pre-write, in-txn) checks all canonical tables present + every writer-populated column exists + no unsatisfiable NOT-NULL drift (tolerates additive ALTER drift), profile_id required. Moved validate before INSERTs for clean errors. WRITTEN_COLUMNS contract in writer.py. 7 unit + 1 integration test; suite 34 green.

title: Export API endpoint
description: POST endpoint to create a project from staged edits; returns backup path + summary.
close_reason: Export API landed: POST /api/export (ProjectSpec of pre-expanded targets -> export_project -> ExportResult w/ backup_path+ids+counts) + POST /api/export/{op}/undo; ValidationError->422, busy/progress->409, ExportError->400. Registered in main.py. Writes to safe staging (live stays env-gated, not exposed). 3 TestClient tests; suite 37 green.

title: Frontend: create project from mosaic + pick exposure plan
description: Turn a framed mosaic into project+targets, choose exposure plan/template, trigger export.
close_reason: Frontend Save flow shipped: ProjectBuilder now has NINA-profile field (datalist from existing projects) + exposure-plan editor (filter/exposure/desired); Save button enabled when valid -> expands each mosaic group via mosaicPanels into per-pane targets, POSTs ProjectSpec to /api/export, shows result/error. Writes to staging. 10-check Playwright E2E (verify-save.mjs). tsc/lint clean.

title: Fix Docker deploy: empty API base + backup DB picked over real schedulerdb
description: Two deploy-only bugs found on first production Docker run: (1) frontend Dockerfile bakes VITE_API_BASE='' and api.ts uses ?? so API_BASE collapses to '' -> same-origin calls return index.html -> JSON.parse error + empty UI; (2) find_source_db globs *.sqlite sorted[0] and picks NINA's dated *-backup.sqlite over the real schedulerdb.sqlite. Also clean up stale STAGING/LIVE config docs left from ur0.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #31 (merged): API base ||-fallback + canonical DB resolution + stale config cleanup

title: Writer leaves NINA-required project columns NULL → projects fail to load in Target Scheduler
description:
REPRO: Save a project (live or staging) and open the DB in NINA Target Scheduler. Exposure templates load, but NO projects appear; the DB 'won't load cleanly'. Confirmed on the user's live DB 2026-06-20 (project Id 26, guid 7781b68b-0a34-401b-b2ce-8a3fdc90c716, live op op_1a760d53...).

ROOT CAUSE: writer.write_project inserts only {profileId,name,description,state,priority,isMosaic,guid} into the project table. It leaves these 8 columns NULL: createdate, minimumtime, minimumaltitude, usecustomhorizon, horizonoffset, meridianwindow, filterswitchfrequency, ditherevery, enablegrader. They are NULLABLE in the SQLite schema (so validate.py's NOT-NULL-without-default check passes them), but NINA's Target Scheduler EF model maps them to NON-nullable .NET types. When EF materializes the projects list and hits a NULL, the whole query throws -> every project disappears (exposuretemplate is a separate, fully-populated query, so templates still load). target/exposureplan rows are unaffected (no null mismatches; unusedOEO is NULL in NINA's own rows).

FIX: populate the project INSERT with NINA's Project defaults: minimumtime=30, minimumaltitude=0.0, usecustomhorizon=0, horizonoffset=0.0, meridianwindow=0, filterswitchfrequency=0, ditherevery=0, enablegrader=1, createdate=int(unix seconds now). Add these to writer.WRITTEN_COLUMNS['project'] so validate confirms the target has them. Add a regression test asserting a freshly written project has NO NULL in any column NINA requires (compare against schema / a known NINA row).

DATA RECOVERY (user's live DB, separate from the code fix): pre-write backup data/backups/schedulerdb-20260620T014518Z-f7f1d3f1.sqlite is intact. Either restore it (discards the new project) or UPDATE project Id 26 to fill the 8 columns. User opted to self-recover.

SEVERITY: P1 — silently corrupts the user-facing view of a LIVE Target Scheduler DB; the additive/backup safety held (data was recoverable) but the written row is malformed.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #17: writer now populates NINA-required project columns + seeds the 8 default ruleweights. Verified working in NINA by user.

title: Edit existing Draft projects with no captured subframes
description:
Allow editing/modifying existing Target Scheduler projects (and their targets/exposure-plans) from TS Assistant — but ONLY when the project is in Draft state AND has no progress (no acquiredimage rows and every exposureplan.acquired==0). This is the modify/update path beyond the current additive-only create flow (mh3.5).

The safety guard already exists: has_progress(conn, project_id) in app/db/provenance.py (built in mh3.2) was added precisely as the guardrail so we never touch started work — reuse it here. The export orchestrator (app/db/export.py) already provides backup + BEGIN IMMEDIATE + validation + provenance + integrity assertions to build on.

Scope sketch:
- Backend: an update path (UPDATE/DELETE within the same transactional+backup wrapper) restricted to rows of a Draft, no-progress project; refuse otherwise (raise the existing ProgressError pattern). Decide whether edits are tracked in ts_assistant_provenance or treated as user-owned.
- API: a PUT/PATCH endpoint (or extend /api/export) to update an existing project from staged edits.
- Frontend: load an existing project into the ProjectBuilder draft (prefill name/profile/targets/panes/exposure-plans), edit, then Save = update instead of create. Only offer Edit on rows where state==draft and no subframes.

Note: this relaxes the strict additive-only contract for these specific safe rows, so it needs its own careful design — keep backup + transactional + the progress gate, and clearly distinguish 'edit my draft' from 'never touch started/active work'.
notes: VERIFIED IN REAL NINA (2026-06-20): user confirmed a full round trip — edit a target in TS Assistant -> export staging DB -> import into NINA -> loads cleanly (after the createDate self-heal fix + clearing the polluted staging file). PR #25 ready to merge.
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #25; verified in real NINA (edit target -> export -> import loads cleanly). In-place edit of Draft no-progress projects + createDate self-heal hardening. Follow-ups filed: psq (creation-side NINA parity), 4dw (staging re-seed).

title: Expand named-object catalog: more off-disk galaxies + named dark nebulae (LDN/Barnard)
description:
Add more named objects to the sky-view overlay. Two gaps:
1. Sparse regions away from the Milky Way disk (high galactic latitude) — add more named galaxies to fill that empty space.
2. No dark nebulae — add a curated selection of named dark nebulae (Lynds LDN and/or Barnard catalogs).
Keep it tastefully uncluttered (curate/size-filter), per the user: 'nice that it's not super cluttered, but we could have some more.'

Current pipeline: scripts/gen_named_objects.py pulls from authoritative catalogs into frontend/src/sky/skyObjects.generated.json (committed; runtime stays offline). Sources today: OpenNGC (Messier complete, Caldwell, IC size-filtered), VizieR VII/20 (Sharpless Sh2, SH2_MIN=30'), VizieR VII/284 (SNR, SNR_MIN=20'), plus a hand-curated FEATURED NGC-galaxy showpiece list. Type model in frontend/src/sky/skyObjects.ts: Catalog = 'M'|'C'|'IC'|'Sh2'|'SNR'|'NGC'; kind = 'galaxy'|'nebula'|'cluster'|'planetary'|'supernova'.

Scope:
- Galaxies: broaden the galaxy selection from OpenNGC (type=G), size-filtered and ideally weighted toward high |galactic latitude| (away from the disk) where the overlay is currently empty; or extend the FEATURED list with well-known off-disk galaxies. Pick a size/brightness threshold that adds density without clutter.
- Dark nebulae: add a new source — VizieR VII/7A (Lynds LDN, has area) and/or VII/220A (Barnard). Curate to famous/large named ones (e.g. B33 Horsehead, B72 Snake, B68, B142/143 Barnard's E, LDN 1235 Dark Shark, etc.). Note dark nebulae mostly sit along the disk (that is where the dust is), so they complement rather than fill the off-disk void.
- Type/render updates: add Catalog tags (e.g. 'LDN','B') and a 'dark' kind to skyObjects.ts; give dark nebulae sensible sizeArcmin so the extent-circle + zoom-aware declutter behave; consider a distinct label/circle color for dark nebulae in AladinView.tsx. Update the generator docstring + skyObjects.ts header comment list.

Verification: re-run the generator (needs outbound HTTPS to GitHub raw + CDS VizieR), commit the regenerated JSON, npm run build/lint, and eyeball the overlay at a few zooms (off-disk galaxies appear; dark nebulae render with labels; not cluttered).

title: Coverage-area mosaic: drag a desired FOV rectangle, auto-divide into rig-FOV panels

Aladin Lite's rectangular select mode is the likely primitive. Future; builds on uhz.3-5 mosaic framing.
close_reason: Coverage-area mosaic shipped: Cover area mode drags an AoI rectangle, un-projects its screen corners via pix2world, and coverageToGrid() auto-divides it into rig-FOV panes (center+size+rotation from the AoI). Frontend-only. Verified geomcheck.mjs 24/24 + verify-p2.mjs 12/12 over the LAN origin.

title: Audit + remove dev-only one-time-migration/compat code that can't occur in real usage
description:
Sweep the codebase for 'legacy'/compat/migration code that was added to paper over a ONE-TIME DEVELOPMENT event (e.g. a rename or refactor done in an unreleased branch) and that can never happen for a real user. Such code is permanent cruft + carries its own tests for a scenario that won't recur.

Concrete example that prompted this (already reverted): the 'plan groups -> exposure plan templates' rename also renamed the app-side store file (data/plan_groups.json -> data/plan_templates.json). I added a self-cleaning _migrate_legacy() + 2 tests so the dev's existing definition survived — but the rename never shipped, so no real user ever had plan_groups.json. The right fix was a one-time on-disk file move (done), not permanent handler code.

What to look for:
- Functions/branches named *_legacy / _migrate* / 'compat' / 'fallback' / 'one-time' that guard against states only reachable mid-development.
- Reading two filenames/schemas because one was renamed pre-release.
- Defaults/shims for data shapes that only existed on a dev's machine.
- Tests that exist solely to cover such migration shims.

Deliverable: a list of such spots with a recommendation (delete vs keep), and remove the clearly-unnecessary ones. Keep genuine migrations that real released users could hit. Guideline: prefer a one-time manual/ops action over permanent code for unreleased-only transitions.

Discovered 2026-06-20 (user feedback: don't over-complicate; no custom handler code + tests for one-time dev-only filename changes).
close_reason: Done with PR #26: removed the dead mode/staging machinery + obsolete config knobs; generalized test-env-specific comments. Kept safety self-heals (createDate/ruleweight/flathistory) per owner.

title: Show acquisition completion metric on project/target list + per-filter in target popup
description:
Surface how complete each target's acquisition is, so the user can see progress at a glance.

LIST (ProjectList.tsx): show a per-target (and ideally per-project rollup) 'completion' summary metric: '(desired subs / accepted subs / pending subs)' where pending = desired - accepted. Compact, alongside the existing target name/coords.

TARGET POPUP (AladinView.tsx target-marker click popup): show the fuller breakdown FILTER BY FILTER — one line per exposure plan: filter name, desired / acquired / accepted (and pending). i.e. the per-plan detail behind the summary number.

Data already exists — mostly a frontend display task: the API ExposurePlan model already carries desired/acquired/accepted (db/models.py, db/reader.py load_projects_conn projects them; api.ts ExposurePlan has desired/acquired/accepted). So no schema/reader changes needed; aggregate per target = sum over its exposure_plans.

Decisions to make during implementation:
- 'pending' definition: desired - accepted (frames still needed to hit the goal) vs desired - acquired (frames not yet captured). Probably desired-accepted (accepted = passed grading) since that's the real 'done' count; consider showing acquired-but-not-accepted (rejected/pending grading) too.
- Whether to roll the summary up to the Project level (sum across its targets) in addition to per-target.
- Visual treatment (e.g. 'L 30/28/2' per filter, or a small progress bar).

Discovered 2026-06-19 (user request).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #19: per-target/per-project acquisition completion badges + per-filter popup breakdown; collapsible Projects accordion. Verified 15/15.

title: Upgrade NSNS surveys from DR0.1 to DR0.2
description: Switch NSNS to the newer DR0.2 release. Verified DR0.2 HiPS layers (all CORS '*', base https://simg.de/nebulae3/dr0_2/<layer>): hbr8 (Ha+continuum color), halpha8 (Ha), oiii8 ([OIII]), sii8 ([SII]), rgb8 (RGB continuum). DR0.2 ADDS [OIII] and [SII] vs DR0.1; tc8 (true color) and sho8 are 404 (absent in DR0.2). Action: update backend app/api/surveys.py NSNS entries to dr0_2 paths and add OIII/SII/RGB variants; decide whether to keep DR0.1 tc8 for true-color. Verify rendering headlessly. File: backend/app/api/surveys.py.
close_reason: Done: NSNS surveys now use DR0.2 (https://simg.de/nebulae3/dr0_2/<layer>): hbr8, halpha8, oiii8, sii8, rgb8 — adds [OIII], [SII], RGB continuum. True Color kept on DR0.1 (no tc8 in DR0.2). Verified headless: all DR0.2 layers load CORS-clean and [OIII] renders over NGC 7000.

title: Equipment editor: Save does not persist changes (empty-id bug)
description: Repro: click + (creates 'New Rig'), rename it / change settings, click Save -> changes are NOT saved. Likely root cause: EquipmentPanel.add() calls createEquipment({id:'', ...BLANK}) which POSTs an EMPTY string id. Backend EquipmentInput uses the provided '' rather than minting one (pydantic default_factory only fires when the field is ABSENT), so profiles get stored with id=''. Save then does PUT /equipment/{id} with an empty id, which does not match the /equipment/{profile_id} route (404), and the rejected promise is unhandled so no error surfaces. Fixes: (a) frontend createEquipment should omit id; (b) backend POST should always generate a fresh id (ignore/!require client id) — make EquipmentInput.id Optional and mint on create; (c) add error handling in EquipmentPanel.save() so failures are visible. Files: frontend/src/panels/EquipmentPanel.tsx, frontend/src/api.ts, backend/app/api/equipment.py, backend/app/equipment.py.
close_reason: Fixed: POST /equipment now mints a fresh id (empty client id was being stored, breaking the subsequent PUT). Added error surfacing in EquipmentPanel save/add/delete. Verified headless: create -> rename -> Save -> reload -> persisted.

title: Collapse staging/live modes into one in-place write path (backup-before-write)
description:
Simplify the write path to a SINGLE consistent mode: read and write the one Target Scheduler DB the user specifies (TS_ASSISTANT_DB / auto-detected), making a consistent backup before every write — exactly the backup we take today. Remove the staging-vs-production duality entirely.

RATIONALE: backups (not a separate staging clone) are the real safety mechanism, and the dual-mode design is the root cause of two bug classes — 4dw (staging export DB goes stale/accumulates; caused a real NINA createDate crash) and the old 2ij (saved projects vanish on reload because reads load the source but writes go to staging). One file for read+write makes saves persist immediately and deletes a lot of complexity.

REMOVE: STAGING vs LIVE branching; allow_live_write() gate + TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE env; _seed_export_db / staging clone + EXPORT_DIR/EXPORT_DB; _resolve_target dual logic; _is_live_target; the _probe_live startup probe + live_error; the red 'PRODUCTION' viewport banner and the staging banner. Replace the banner with a small neutral status line (which DB file is being written + backup location).

MUST PRESERVE (the actual safety, mostly already in export.py): consistent WAL-aware pre-write backup (backup.ensure_backup, window 0); BEGIN IMMEDIATE with fast busy->DatabaseBusyError 409 ('pause/close NINA'); integrity quick_check before locking; PRAGMA foreign_key_check + additive assertions; ts_assistant_provenance + undo_operation; has_progress guard; backup pruning/retention; keep the held write-lock window sub-second so a live NINA just gets a clean 409. Reads use WAL (readers don't block); resolves read-after-write so the UI reflects saves on reload.

SCOPE NOTES: /api/health 'mode'/'read_path'/'write_target'/'live_error' shape simplifies — coordinate the frontend banner + README (TurquoiseSpring's staging-vs-live section gets rewritten; ping after). This SUPERSEDES bead 4dw (no staging file to go stale) and makes the 2ij resolution the default. Work alongside ry6 in the cleanup pass. Verify with the real-machine concurrency gate from PR #15 (mid-session backup has latest rows; concurrent writer gets clean 409; held lock sub-second).
close_reason: Shipped & merged via PR #26. Single in-place write path (read+write the configured DB, backup before every write); removed STAGING/LIVE duality. Verified in app: writes persist on reload, backups taken.

title: Label each target box with the target name (frame-matched color, bottom edge)
description:
Render the target's name on each target box in the sky view for at-a-glance identification.

Current state: frontend/src/sky/AladinView.tsx draws target boxes as Aladin graphicOverlay polygons with no text labels — per-target FOV boxes in cyan (#00e5ff), project-draft boxes in amber (#ffb300), coverage overlay white (#ffffff). Named-object labels exist but are for catalog objects, not target boxes.

Request:
- Label each target box with its target name.
- Color the label to match that box's frame color (cyan for FOV boxes, amber for draft boxes, etc.).
- Place the label along the BOTTOM edge of the box.

Implementation notes:
- Compute the box's bottom-edge midpoint from the polygon corners (account for rotation) and place the label there; or use Aladin's overlay/source label support with matching color.
- Keep the existing zoom-aware declutter in mind (labels should not clutter at wide FOV) — reuse/extend the current declutter logic.
- Applies to both the per-target FOV overlay and the project-draft overlay; thread the target name (already available on the render entries) through to the label.
